name: FLORENTINE GROTESCHI #13
description: Florentine Groteschi #13 is a glitch candy rot single-panel relic built around Botticelli and Webring Validator Shrine. Tarocchi Fiorentini assigns Sette di Coppe, used here as the witness card. A Solomonic-style seal copied into a Florentine manuscript margin gets pulled into a receipt validator for sacred paperwork, making the panel read like a corrupted receipt for belief, status, and patronage. The groupchat says no cap and immediately screenshots the evidence. The generated insert, a pixelated espresso cup that steams private keys, acts as the modern clue; Gematria marks RATIO at Σ 378.

attributes.value: a pixelated espresso cup that steams private keys

florentine_groteschi.collector_note: Custom top-level lore data: not an OpenSea trait list, but intentionally readable in the raw token JSON.

chess_piece.name: Queen

rarity.ranking_rule: Higher chess strength/rank is intentionally rarer: Pawn common, Knight/Bishop uncommon, Rook rare, Queen epic, King legendary.

board_position.position_rule: Deterministic per-token board square reserved for future game/use mechanics; independent of visible glyph placement.

chess_piece.allocation_rule: Weighted deterministic shuffled chess allocation by piece strength: {'Pawn': 560, 'Knight': 280, 'Bishop': 280, 'Rook': 168, 'Queen': 84, 'King': 29}
chess_piece.metadata_visibility: Hidden from OpenSea attributes; stored only in florentine_groteschi.hidden_traits.
chess_piece.visual_rule: A small unlabeled foreground chess glyph shows the chess-piece graphic with the piece name encoded underneath; that visible code is intentionally not serialized in metadata.

visible_text.title: FLORENTINE GROTESCHI #13
visible_text.main_caption: Every pop-up looks like a tiny heresy trial. The panel opens as a corrupted case file: a Solomonic-style seal copied into a Florentine manuscript margin has to answer to a receipt validator for sacred paperwork; the groupchat says no cap and immediately screenshots the evidence.
visible_text.history_now_bridge: WORKSHOP BUG REPORT: a Solomonic-style seal copied into a Florentine manuscript margin gets pulled into a receipt validator for sacred paperwork. Sette di Coppe reads the jump as patronage theater: who certifies the relic, who pays for belief, and who gets the receipt. The groupchat says no cap and immediately screenshots the evidence.

artwork_breakdown.generated_insert: a pixelated espresso cup that steams private keys

tarocchi_fiorentini.allocation_rule: 14 complete 97-card sets across 1,401 tokens, plus 43 deterministic extras

artwork_breakdown.composition_read: Botticelli anchors the historical source. Sette di Coppe supplies the witness logic. The pixelated espresso cup that steams private keys acts as the modern meme-object. The glitch candy rot palette and Webring Validator Shrine styling make the panel read like a corrupted Renaissance case file.

groteschi_explanation.plain_english: Read plainly: Botticelli is treated as evidence in a workshop bug report. The old-world clue is a Solomonic-style seal copied into a Florentine manuscript margin; the modern-world echo is a receipt validator for sacred paperwork. The joke is that patronage, holy paperwork, and marketplace verification are all status systems asking who gets believed.
groteschi_explanation.read_between_the_lines: The Sette di Coppe card is not decorative; it tells you how to read the room. The old clue and the modern clue compete as evidence, so the panel becomes a custody argument about belief, status, and receipts. The groupchat says no cap and immediately screenshots the evidence. The slang turns sacred authority into aura, patronage into clout economics, and marginalia into groupchat evidence.

hidden_solution.method: Split the visible marker into DNA triplets, translate each triplet through the genetic-code table, then read the amino-acid letters as the source Base name. Non-standard letters use documented stop/nearest triplet placeholders.

hidden_acrostic.method: Read the first character of each acrostic line; slashes are spaces.

collector_warnings: Sette di Coppe is a witness, not a decorative rectangle.
Do not let the codon marker breed near the provenance strip.
Opening the metadata counts as participation in the trial.
Do not cite the notary after midnight.
If the ledger balances, something has gone terribly wrong.
